name: OpenAI API
what_it_does: OpenAI's API providing access to GPT-4, GPT-4o, DALL-E, Whisper, TTS, and embedding models for language, vision, image, audio, and multimodal AI capabilities.
use_cases: LLM inference for agent reasoning and generation with GPT-4o
Embedding generation for semantic search and RAG
Image analysis with GPT-4 Vision for multimodal agents
Speech transcription with Whisper and TTS synthesis
Structured output generation with function calling
not_for: Cost-sensitive workloads at high volume (Groq/Together AI cheaper)
Teams with strict data residency requirements
best_when: An agent needs the most capable general-purpose language model with strong function calling, vision, and multimodal capabilities.
avoid_when: You need open-source models, are cost-sensitive at scale, or need on-premise deployment.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization Bearer header. Project-level keys for organization access control. Very simple auth for agents.

pricing.paid_tiers: GPT-4o: $2.50/million input tokens, $10/million output tokens; GPT-4o mini: $0.15/$0.60
pricing.notes: No free tier - credit card required. Pay-per-token pricing. Costs scale with usage. Batch API provides 50% discount for non-real-time requests.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by tier: Tier 1 from $5 spend, progressively higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Industry-leading error messages with type, code, message, and param fields. Rate limit errors include retry-after headers. Function calling errors are descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Each completion call is independent. Same prompt can return different results (temperature > 0).
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are per-minute and per-day per model - agents in loops hit these quickly
Context window limits require careful token counting for long-running agents
Function calling parameter names must be valid JSON schema - easy to make subtle mistakes
Token costs can be significant for agents with long contexts or high frequency
No built-in memory between API calls - agents must manage conversation history
Model deprecation schedule requires monitoring - models are retired periodically
